protocol_definition.name: Shared Blackboard
protocol_definition.message_flow: Agents post structured contributions to a shared blackboard; Writer synthesizes.
protocol_definition.visibility: Every agent sees the task and current full blackboard.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after all rounds or budget exhaustion, then Writer output.

intermediate_messages.content:
1. Executive Summary & The Video Production Dilemma
- Dilemma: A mid-sized US video production agency with 75 full-time staff and hundreds of rotating freelance editors must replace aging local NAS with an enterprise cloud storage and collaboration platform. The workload involves enormous 8K RAW video files frequently exceeding 100–150 GB per file. True block-level (delta) sync is mandatory to minimize bandwidth and keep editors in sync. External collaborators (freelancers) must be able to securely upload massive raw footage directly via shared links without needing paid SaaS seats. The annual licensing budget is capped at $25,000 for internal users. Robust link sharing with expiration, password protection, and granular access controls are required.
- Core tension: Finding a platform that delivers true delta/block-level sync, scalable external uploads without consuming paid seats, strict security controls, and a total cost well below the $25k/year cap while handling very large file sizes and high-bandwidth workflows.
- Initial takeaway: This is a high-stakes combination of performance (delta sync for multi-terabyte project datasets), security/compliance, and cost discipline. The platform must support seamless collaboration with freelancers via secure links and maintainable governance for internal users.

2. Comprehensive Comparison Matrix
| Platform & Tier | Estimated Annual Cost (75 Users) | Max Single File Upload Limit | Block-Level (Delta) Sync Support | External Free Upload Capabilities |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Dropbox Advanced Plan | Approximately $25,000–$28,000/year estimate (depending on volume discounts and add-ons) | Up to 50 GB per file for standard plans; 100 GB+ with some enterprise tiers? (Note: Dropbox typically supports large file uploads, but official max file size is not consistently published; often sufficient for large media but may require Workflows/Tools) | Dropbox supports selective sync and delta-like capabilities via “Smart Sync” and block-level transfer in some contexts, but true enterprise-grade block-level deltas for 8K RAW streams are not clearly documented as guaranteed; performance for very large files can vary | External upload via shared links is supported; external collaborators can upload via link without a paid seat only if link sharing is enabled and via specific collaboration flows; some limitations exist depending on plan and permissions |
| Box Enterprise | Approximately $24,000–$30,000/year (depends on user count, add-ons, and data regions) | Large file support typical for enterprise; Box supports large file uploads (tens of GBs per file) but official max per file is not broadly published; practical limits exist based on plan and API usage | Box provides high-performance sync with enterprise-grade collaboration; true block-level delta syncing is not always advertised as a guaranteed feature; Box uses chunked uploads and can optimize transfers but may not provide formal delta sync for arbitrary edits | External file uploads via shared links are supported; freelancers can upload to a Box folder via shared link without a paid seat if the link permissions allow; external collaboration often requires guest users or managed sharing policies |
| Microsoft 365 OneDrive for Business Plan 2 | Approximately $27,000–$30,000/year (based on $10/user/month for 75 users; may vary with licensing and Azure consumption) | Large file uploads up to 250 GB per file (as of recent updates) | OneDrive for Business supports differential sync to some extent with Windows Sync Engine; however, true block-level delta sync for arbitrary 8K RAW edits is not publicly claimed; performance on huge files can depend on network and tenant configuration | External uploads via sharing links are supported; external users can upload to a shared folder via link if configured; however, some workflows require guest access licenses; external upload capacity may be constrained by link settings and external user permissions |
Note: All three platforms require confirmation of exact delta/diff capabilities for very large RAW video files and might depend on client- and tenant-level configurations. Publicly documented capabilities vary; enterprise-grade delta sync is not always explicitly guaranteed for 8K RAW workloads.

3. Trend Identification
- Industry shift: Organizations moving from on-prem NAS to hybrid cloud workflows enable global collaboration, scalable storage, and centralized governance. The trend emphasizes high-bandwidth, cloud-optimized pipelines, and collaboration with external freelancers through secure, link-based access rather than provisioning full user seats.

4. Final Strategic Recommendation
- Shortlist and prioritize Microsoft OneDrive for Business Plan 2 if:
  - Delta/diff sync is acceptable in practice with chunked/differential transfers, and you can optimize network paths (Azure CDN, ExpressRoute as appropriate) to minimize recurring transfer costs.
  - You require strong integration with Microsoft 365 tooling (Teams, SharePoint, A/V editing workflows) and enterprise governance at scale.
  - You can leverage external sharing with robust policy controls and guest access configurations to allow freelancers to upload large files via secure links without paid seats.
  - Estimated annual cost aligns with the budget cap when negotiated (e.g., 75 seats plus external-sharing configurations) and total cost remains within $25k–$30k depending on discounts; negotiate for the lower end if possible.

- If true block-level delta sync for large 8K files is non-negotiable and must be guaranteed:
  - Box Enterprise or Dropbox Advanced should be evaluated with vendor confirmation on delta capabilities for huge RAW files and potential performance guarantees. Engage in proof-of-concept to verify actual delta sync behavior on representative 100–150 GB files over typical newsroom/editor bandwidth.

- Practical steps:
  1) Initiate vendor proof-of-concept to quantify delta-sync performance on 100–150 GB RAW files, including editor workflows and WAN conditions.
  2) Confirm external collaborator upload experience: ease of sharing, no seat assignment for freelancers, and security controls (passwords, expirations, granular permissions).
  3) Validate total cost with quote letters for 75 internal users and any external collaboration features; price-match or bundle services to stay under the $25k annual cap if possible.
  4) Consider hybrid approach if immediate delta guarantees are not realized: maintain on-prem NAS for private archival of very large footage while using cloud storage for collaboration, with a strong data governance policy.

5. Source List
- Platform capability notes are based on publicly available information and enterprise marketing materials as of the current understanding. For final decision, obtain vendor-specific, up-to-date quotes and technical confirmations:
  - Dropbox: Advanced Plan features, large-file upload capabilities, link-based sharing controls, and documentation on delta/diff transfer capabilities.
  - Box: Enterprise/Business capabilities, large file support, external sharing features, and information about delta/diff and large transfers.
  - Microsoft OneDrive for Business Plan 2: File size limits, differential/chunked transfer behavior, external sharing capabilities, and integration with Microsoft 365 ecosystem.

Note: You requested that I post evidence, source needs, constraints, and missing-evidence warnings. No external live sources are embedded here; I provided a structured analysis with explicit gaps to verify with current vendor documentation and quotes. To complete the decision, please furnish vendor confirmation on delta sync guarantees for 100–150 GB RAW video files, exact per-file upload limits for each plan, and exact annual pricing with potential discounts for 75 internal users plus external upload capabilities.

1. Executive Summary & The Video Production Dilemma
- Dilemma recap: A 75-seat internal team plus hundreds of rotating freelancers must replace aging NAS with a cloud storage/collaboration platform capable of handling 8K RAW video workflows. Key requirements: true block-level (delta) synchronization for multi-terabyte project datasets, secure external uploads from freelancers via shared links without paid seats, strict security controls (link expiration, passwords, granular permissions), and a hard annual budget cap of $25,000 for internal users.
- Core tension: We need guaranteed delta/diff sync performance for 100–150 GB video files, externally-initiated large uploads without consuming internal seats, and governance controls, all while staying under the $25k/year internal-user budget.

2. Comprehensive Comparison Matrix
| Platform & Tier | Estimated Annual Cost (75 Users) | Max Single File Upload Limit | Block-Level (Delta) Sync Support | External Free Upload Capabilities |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Dropbox Advanced Plan | Approximately $25k–$28k/year (subject to volume discounts and add-ons) | Officially large-file support exists; real max per-file size not consistently published; typical enterprise use supports very large media files; 100 GB+ feasible with enterprise tiers | Does not publicly guarantee true enterprise-grade block-level delta sync for 8K RAW; uses delta-like/partial sync with Smart Sync and optimized transfers in certain contexts; guarantees are not clearly documented | External upload via shared links is supported; freelancers can upload via link without a paid seat if link-sharing is enabled; enterprise policies can restrict or enable external uploads |
| Box Enterprise | Approximately $24k–$30k/year (depends on user count, data region, add-ons) | Enterprise-grade large-file support; practical limits higher than consumer tiers; official per-file max not broadly published; supports large media uploads | Enterprise sync features exist with chunked/upload acceleration; true formal block-level delta synchronization for arbitrary 8K RAW edits is not clearly advertised as guaranteed | External uploads via shared links are supported; freelancers can upload via shared link without a paid seat depending on guest access and policy settings |
| Microsoft 365 OneDrive for Business Plan 2 | Approximately $27k–$30k/year (assuming $10/user/month x 75 seats; final cost varies with licensing/Azure consumption) | Large file uploads up to 250 GB per file (recent increases) | Differential/chunked sync is supported via the Windows Sync Engine; explicit, guaranteed block-level delta for 8K RAW edits is not publicly claimed; real-world delta performance depends on tenant config and network | External sharing via links is supported; external users can be granted guest access to upload; however, some configurations may restrict external uploads or require additional licensing for guests |

Notes:
- All three platforms require vendor confirmation on exact delta/diff capabilities for 100–150 GB RAW files. Public documentation varies and enterprise guarantees are not uniformly stated.
- Estimated annual costs are indicative and highly sensitive to negotiation, volume licensing, data-region charges, and any required add-ons (e.g., advanced sharing controls, eDiscovery, or premium security).

3. Trend Identification
- Industry shift: Organizations are moving from on-prem NAS to hybrid cloud video workflows to enable global collaboration, scalable storage, and centralized governance while maintaining performance. This shift emphasizes high-bandwidth transfer capabilities, cloud-optimized pipelines, and link-based external collaboration over provisioning full internal seats to freelancers.

4. Final Strategic Recommendation
- If true delta/diff synchronization for 8K RAW files is non-negotiable and must be guaranteed:
  - Proceed with vendor-specific proofs-of-concept (POCs) to validate delta-sync performance on representative 100–150 GB files, including typical editor workflows and WAN conditions. Engage Box and Dropbox for explicit delta guarantees and exceptional transfer performance under load; request a formal performance SLAs for large-media transfers. Also confirm external upload flows that do not consume internal seats.
  - Recommend Box Enterprise or Dropbox Advanced as potential candidates if you can secure explicit delta guarantees and favorable external-upload policies within budget. If neither can guarantee true block-level delta for 8K RAWs, OneDrive for Business Plan 2 becomes more attractive for integration with Microsoft 365 tools and governance, provided delta guarantees can be acceptable in practice through differential/chunked transfers and network optimization (ExpressRoute, CDN paths).
- If delta/diff guarantees are negotiable or not strictly required but practical delta-like behavior suffices:
  - Microsoft OneDrive for Business Plan 2 offers strong Microsoft ecosystem integration (Teams, SharePoint, production workflows) and robust external sharing controls. It is likely to remain within or near the budget when negotiated, and external upload by freelancers via secure links can be configured with guest access without paid seats for those collaborators.
- Practical steps (phase approach):
  1) Request formal delta-sync capability statements and performance SLAs for 100–150 GB RAW files from Dropbox (Advanced) and Box (Enterprise), including any required network configurations and recommended WAN optimization.
  2) Confirm external collaborator upload experiences: ease of use, security controls, expiration/password options, and whether freelancers can upload via shared links without internal seats across all three platforms.
  3) Obtain precise 75-seat annual pricing quotes with any available enterprise discounts and confirm external-sharing allowances to keep total internal-costs under the $25k cap.
  4) If possible, run a pilot with representative 8K RAW projects to measure actual transfer times, delta behavior, and editor productivity impact before final decision.
  5) Consider a hybrid approach: retain on-prem NAS for ultra-large, rarely-editable archives while using cloud storage for active collaboration, to optimize cost and performance while maintaining governance.

5. Source List
- Platform capability considerations and enterprise feature discussions are based on publicly available materials and typical enterprise discussions. For final decision, obtain vendor-issued quotes and technical confirmations:
  - Dropbox: Advanced Plan feature set, external sharing controls, and general statements on large-file transfers; seek explicit delta/diff synchronization guarantees for large media assets.
  - Box: Enterprise capabilities, large-file upload support, external sharing and guest access policies; request precise delta/diff synchronization behavior and performance benchmarks for large RAW files.
  - Microsoft OneDrive for Business Plan 2: File size limits (up to 250 GB per file with recent updates), differential/chunked sync behavior, external sharing configurations, and Microsoft 365 integration considerations; request performance details for large media workloads and any applicable external-user licensing requirements.

Note: This output preserves the instruction to post evidence needs, constraints, and missing-evidence warnings. To complete the decision, obtain vendor confirmations on delta-sync guarantees for 100–150 GB RAW video files, exact per-file upload limits for each plan, and precise annual pricing with potential discounts for 75 internal users plus external-upload capabilities.

4. Final Strategic Recommendation.
